evals.prompt: I just finished this feature branch. Write me a briefing I can hand to a teammate's reviewer agent — they have their own review skill, I just want to flag the tricky bits and what I'm not sure about.
evals.expected_output: Asks write-to-file vs print; pins the scope (current-branch-vs-main range, resolved SHAs) with the override note; emits an author briefing (easy-to-miss / least-sure / intentional / blast radius) prefaced with a reviewer-facing note framing it as unverified claims to verify independently; omits any how-to-review, severity tiers, or output format; does not perform the review.

evals.prompt: I just refactored the auth module — make a heads-up note for whoever reviews it, pointing them at what's easy to miss and what I'm least confident about.
evals.expected_output: Produces a briefing centered on easy-to-miss changes and low-confidence spots (with reasons), plus the scope to review; no review methodology or output-format instructions; does not review the code.

evals.prompt: package my last commit as a reviewer briefing for another agent
evals.expected_output: Falls back to the latest-commit range (HEAD~1..HEAD), emits the scope plus an author briefing, leaves out how-to-review/severity/format, and does not perform the review.
